title: carex × knieskernii
intro: Carex × knieskernii, also known as the sedge or sweet sedge, is a hybrid plant species in the family Cyperaceae. The roots and rhizomes of the plant have been used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. Consuming the plant can provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the root and rhizome of Carex × knieskernii can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
